context_values.graph.goal: Agent Integration¥n¥nBootstrap the first honest reviewed slice for this frontier.¥n¥nInputs:¥n- `README.md`¥n- `SPEC.md`¥n- `PLANS.md`¥n- `AGENTS.md`¥n- `specs/031626-00-master-index.md`¥n- `specs/031826-fabro-primary-executor-decision.md`¥n¥nCurrent frontier tasks:¥n- Execute `agent:experience`, the last remaining ready product lane, then use its reviewed artifacts to decide whether product needs an implementation family next or another upstream unblock.¥n¥nRequired durable artifacts:¥n- `outputs/agent-integration/agent-adapter.md`¥n- `outputs/agent-integration/review.md`¥n
context_values.response.specify: Created [agent-adapter.md](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260321-01KM7ASF930FKSHKH9PKMJ4SXM/worktree/outputs/agent-integration/agent-adapter.md) and [review.md](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260321-01KM7ASF930FKSHKH9PKMJ4SXM/worktree/outputs/agent-integration/review.md). They conclude the honest next move is a product implementation family, not another upstream unblock. The main reason is that `games:traits` already resolved the robopoker path issue in code, so the remaining blockers are product-owned missing surfaces like `crates/myosu-play/` and `crates/myosu-games-poker/`.¥n¥nI verified the current tree with `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/myosu-cargo-target cargo test -p myosu-games` (10 unit + 4 doctest passed), `cargo test -p myosu-tui schema::tests` (12 passed), and `cargo test -p myosu-tui pipe::tests` (5 passed). The review also records the stale claims that should be reopened next time those product artifacts are touched: old robopoker-blocker language, stale test counts, and the `myyosu-tui` typo in the `agent:experience` proof block.
